# Beudox HR - Technical Documentation for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a React-based single-page application (SPA) built with TypeScript and Vite, using Supabase as the backend-as-a-service platform. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Technology Stack
- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design system
- **UI Components**: shadcn/ui (Radix UI primitives)
- **Routing**: React Router DOM v6 with protected routes
- **State Management**: React Query (TanStack Query) for server state
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Real-time)
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Icons**: Lucide React
- **Charts**: Recharts for data visualization
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E

## Key Architectural Decisions

### 1. Component Architecture
- Uses shadcn/ui for consistent, accessible UI components
- Custom layout components (AppLayout, AppSidebar, TopBar) for navigation
- Page-level components for route handling
- Utility-first CSS with Tailwind for styling

### 2. Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth for user authentication
- Custom `useAuth` hook (not yet implemented in codebase)
- Protected routes with automatic redirects
- Row Level Security (RLS) on database tables
- Employee-based access control with company and role relationships

### 3. Data Management
- React Query for caching and synchronization
- Supabase client for database operations
- Type-safe database operations with generated TypeScript types
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates

### 4. Routing Structure
- BrowserRouter with nested routes
- Protected routes wrapped in AppLayout
- Public routes for authentication flows
- 404 handling with NotFound component

## Database Schema & Relationships

### Core Tables
- **employees**: User profiles linked to Supabase auth users
  - Fields: id, auth_user_id, full_name, email, employee_code, designation, department, avatar_url, company_id, status
- **companies**: Organizational entities
  - Fields: id, name, slug
- **roles**: Permission roles within companies
  - Fields: id, name, company_id
- **employee_roles**: Many-to-many relationship
  - Fields: employee_id, role_id

### Security Model
- All tables use Row Level Security (RLS)
- Users can only access data for their own company
- Employees can only view their own records and company-wide data
- Security definer functions for complex access patterns

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, renders App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing and providers

### Layout Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout wrapper with sidebar and content area
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Collapsible navigation sidebar with menu sections
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Page title header bar

### UI Components
- `src/components/ui/`: Complete shadcn/ui component library
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Custom logo component with variants
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: React Router NavLink wrapper with active state styling

### Pages/Routes
- `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`: Main dashboard (protected)
- `src/pages/Login.tsx`: Authentication page
- `src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx`: Password reset request
- `src/pages/ResetPassword.tsx`: Password reset form
- `src/pages/NotFound.tsx`: 404 error page

### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Auto-generated TypeScript types from database schema

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom theme
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

## Business Logic & Data Flow

### Authentication Flow
1. User visits root path `/`
2. `RootRedirect` component checks auth state
3. Unauthenticated users redirected to `/login`
4. Authenticated users redirected to `/dashboard`
5. Protected routes use `ProtectedRoute` wrapper
6. Loading states show spinner during auth checks

### Navigation Structure
- Sidebar organized into sections: MAIN, PEOPLE, FINANCE, WORK, SYSTEM
- Each section contains navigation items with icons and paths
- Active route highlighting with purple accent color
- Collapsible sidebar for mobile responsiveness

### Component Patterns
- Functional components with hooks
- TypeScript interfaces for props
- Custom hooks for reusable logic
- Utility functions in `src/lib/utils.ts`
- Class variance authority for component variants

## Development Workflow

### Build & Development
- `npm run dev`: Start Vite dev server with hot reload
- `npm run build`: Production build with optimization
- `npm run lint`: ESLint for code quality
- `npm run test`: Vitest unit tests
- `npm run test:watch`: Watch mode testing

### Environment Configuration
- Environment variables prefixed with `VITE_`
- Supabase URL and API key required
- Local development uses `.env` file

## Areas for Development

### Incomplete Features
- `useAuth` hook not implemented (imported but file missing)
- Many route components not created (only Dashboard exists)
- Placeholder `Index.tsx` page
- Limited error handling and loading states

### Potential Enhancements
- Implement remaining HR modules (Employees, Attendance, etc.)
- Add comprehensive testing coverage
- Implement real-time notifications
- Add data export/import functionality
- Enhance mobile responsiveness
- Add internationalization (i18n)

## Code Quality Standards

- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- ESLint with React and TypeScript rules
- Prettier for code formatting (implied)
- Component naming conventions (PascalCase)
- File naming conventions (kebab-case for files, PascalCase for components)
- Import organization (external libs, then internal components)
- JSDoc comments for complex functions
- Accessibility considerations with Radix UI

This documentation provides the foundation for understanding the Beudox HR codebase and can be expanded as the application grows.
